text: The satellite caught it at 347 UTC. A vertical wound in the atmosphere, 200 meters tall,
bleeding light the color of deep copper. Through it came shapes, broad, hunched, wrong.
The first analysts thought equipment malfunctioned. Then the shapes stepped onto the frozen lake
and the ice held their weight. Figures that should not exist, carrying weapons that should not glow.
They moved in formation, Nakhayas, not Panic, formation. Someone had trained them.
Someone had sent them. The rift pulsed wider. More came. The snow around them did not melt from
heat, but from something older, something that made the air taste of iron and wet stone.
Humanity had 11 minutes before the first Neanderthal boot touched soil that belonged to someone else.
11 minutes. That was all the warning the world got.
